probe_contract.read: Each producer pass owns only one active-group chunk and accumulates into a smaller shared-memory group_acc. The final DKV is zeroed once, then both chunk passes atomic-add their active-group slice. This halves accumulator rows but duplicates the MFMA contribution producer body.

decision.read: Active-group chunking reduces shared accumulator footprint but loses badly because it recomputes the query-major MFMA contribution body for each active-group chunk. The useful next kernel direction is intra-CTA or wave-local segmented reduction within a single producer pass, so Q/dO/prob loads and MFMA fragments are reused while LDS read-modify-write pressure is reduced.
decision.next_kernel_direction: Prototype wave-private/lane-group accumulation for hot active groups, or move to a lower-level TileLang/HIP schedule that can reduce several selected rows sharing a group before touching group_acc. Re-profile SQ_INSTS_LDS and SQ_LDS_BANK_CONFLICT against the retained 5.1 ms HSK bf16 baseline.
